claim: Less than 10% of the gabonese children with Schimmelpenning-Feuerstein-Mims syndrome (SFM) had a plasma lactate of more than 5mmol/L.

result.claim: Less than 10% of the gabonese children with Schimmelpenning-Feuerstein-Mims syndrome (SFM) had a plasma lactate of more than 5mmol/L.
result.reasoning: A thorough search of the knowledge base for "Gabonese children Schimmelpenning-Feuerstein-Mims syndrome plasma lactate" and related terms did not yield any relevant propositions or chunks of information. The search results were consistently unrelated to Schimmelpenning-Feuerstein-Mims syndrome (SFM) or plasma lactate levels in Gabonese children. Therefore, there is no evidence within the provided knowledge base to support or refute the claim.

evidence.text: Six of 53 children (11%) who received dexamethasone at 0.05 mg/kg had bleeding.
evidence.source: Dexamethasone and risk of nausea and vomiting and postoperative bleeding after tonsillectomy in children: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: The WHO Global Database on Child Growth and Malnutrition includes data on approximately 31 million children younger than 5 years.
evidence.source: Estimates of global prevalence of childhood underweight in 1990 and 2015.

evidence.text: Alpha(+)-thalassaemia is a heritable haemoglobinopathy.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Individuals with panic disorder have marked increases in autonomic and respiratory responses after intravenous infusions of hypertonic sodium lactate.
evidence.source: A KEY ROLE FOR OREXIN IN PANIC ANXIETY

evidence.text: The haematological profile in children homozygous for alpha(+)-thalassaemia reduces the risk of SMA during acute malaria compared to children of normal genotype.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Children received either dexamethasone (0.05, 0.15, or 0.5 mg/kg) or placebo intravenously.
evidence.source: Dexamethasone and risk of nausea and vomiting and postoperative bleeding after tonsillectomy in children: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: Increased erythrocyte count and microcytosis occur in children homozygous for alpha(+)-thalassaemia.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: The blood methylation age of the rhesus monkeys appeared 7 years younger than their chronologic age due to caloric restriction.
evidence.source: Caloric restriction delays age-related methylation drift

evidence.text: Total body water volume (TBW) and extracellular water volume (ECW) were estimated in children with severe or moderate malaria and in convalescence by tracer dilution with heavy water and bromide.
evidence.source: Assessment of Volume Depletion in Children with Malaria

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice were born normal.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Children homozygous for alpha(+)-thalassaemia may have protection against SMA.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Two of 51 children (4%) who received dexamethasone at 0.15 mg/kg had bleeding.
evidence.source: Dexamethasone and risk of nausea and vomiting and postoperative bleeding after tonsillectomy in children: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: Severe childhood malaria was associated with depletion of TBW (mean [SD] of 37 [33] ml/kg, or 6.7% [6.0%]) relative to measurement at discharge.
evidence.source: Assessment of Volume Depletion in Children with Malaria

evidence.text: Twelve of 50 children (24%) who received dexamethasone at 0.5 mg/kg had bleeding.
evidence.source: Dexamethasone and risk of nausea and vomiting and postoperative bleeding after tonsillectomy in children: a randomized trial.

evidence.text: Children with acute falciparum malaria showed a reduction in median erythrocyte count of approximately 1.5 x 10(12)/l relative to values in community children.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Mutations that eliminate LDLRs raise LDL and cause heart attacks in childhood.
evidence.source: A Century of Cholesterol and Coronaries: From Plaques to Genes to Statins

evidence.text: The 95% confidence interval was 0.24-1.12 for the children with alpha(+)-thalassaemia.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: Children homozygous for alpha(+)-thalassaemia require a 10% greater reduction in erythrocyte count than children of normal genotype for Hb concentration to fall to 50 g/l.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: The relative risk was 0.52 for the children with alpha(+)-thalassaemia.
evidence.source: Increased Microerythrocyte Count in Homozygous α+-Thalassaemia Contributes to Protection against Severe Malarial Anaemia

evidence.text: The increased rate of macrosomia in the offspring of pregnant women with diabetes is mediated by increased foetal insulin secretion.
evidence.source: Macrosomia and Hyperinsulinaemic Hypoglycaemia in Patients with Heterozygous Mutations in the HNF4A Gene
